workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws close security group
workflows.guide:
## 🔐 **Subworkflow: Close Traffic to and from an AWS Security Group**

🚫 This subworkflow removes **all ingress and egress rules** from a specified AWS security group, effectively blocking **all inbound and outbound traffic**.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awssecuritygroupid** (Required): 🆔 The ID of the AWS security group to modify.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the security group is located.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document/runbook:  
🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWS-CloseSecurityGroup/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🔗 **Dependencies**

This subworkflow depends on the following subworkflow:

- 🧩 [subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws create ami image
workflows.guide:
## 🖼️ **Subworkflow to Create an AMI Image**

📸 This subworkflow **creates an Amazon Machine Image (AMI) from a running EC2 instance** via the **AWS-CreateImage** SSM automation document.

Useful for backups, creating standardized images, and version control of instance configurations.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 The instance ID to create AMI from.  
- **imageName** (Required): 📝 Name for the new AMI.  
- **imageDescription** (Optional): 📋 Description for the AMI.  
- **noReboot** (Optional): ⚡ Create AMI without rebooting (true/false). Default: false.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document**

**Document Name**: `AWS-CreateImage`

🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-createimage.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Creates an AMI from a running EC2 instance
- Optionally reboots instance during creation
- Captures the instance configuration and attached volumes
- Produces a reusable image for launching new instances

---

🔗 Dependencies
This subworkflow depends on the following subworkflow:

[🧩 subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws create cloudformation stack
workflows.guide:
## ☁️ **Subworkflow: Create an AWS CloudFormation Stack**

🚀 This subworkflow provides a simple and standardized way to trigger the creation of an **AWS CloudFormation stack** using AWS Systems Manager (SSM).

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **cf_stackname** (Required): 🏷️ The name of the CloudFormation stack to create.  
- **cfyamls3url** (Required): 📄 The S3 URL of the CloudFormation YAML template to deploy  
  (must follow this format: `https://s3bucket.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/cf.yaml`).  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the CloudFormation stack will be created.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 📘 **Prerequisites**

✅ This subworkflow requires the custom AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document  
**`Dynwf-CreateCloudFormationStack`** to be available in the AWS region specified by the `awsregion` parameter.

📖 Refer to the **Steps to Import AWS SSM Document / Runbook YAML** section below for setup instructions.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow waits for the SSM document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 🧩 **Workflow Steps**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends a command to create a CloudFormation stack to the  
  AWS SSM document **`Dynwf-CreateCloudFormationStack`**  
- ⏳ It waits for the SSM document execution to complete  
- ✅ The execution result is validated to confirm successful stack creation  
- ❌ If the creation fails, the subworkflow fails to enable **restart-on-error** behavior in the parent workflow

---

### 🔗 **Dependencies**

This subworkflow requires the following subworkflow:

- 🧩 `/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908`

---

## 🛠️ **Steps to Import AWS SSM Document / Runbook YAML**

1. 🔗 Open the following link:  
   https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/create-document?region=us-east-1#documentType=Automation  
   *(You must be logged in to the AWS Management Console.)*

2. 🌍 If needed, change the AWS region in the console to match your target environment.

3. ✏️ In the upper-left corner, set the runbook name to:  
   **`Dynwf-CreateCloudFormationStack`**

4. 🧾 Click the **`{}` Code** button next to the runbook name to open the YAML definition.

5. 📋 Paste the following YAML content into the code editor:

```yaml
schemaVersion: '0.3'

parameters:
  stackname:
    type: String
  cfyamls3url:
    type: String

mainSteps:
  - name: CreateCloudFormationStack
    action: aws:createStack
    isEnd: true
    inputs:
      StackName: '{{ stackname }}'
      Capabilities:
        - CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM
      TimeoutInMinutes: 120
      TemplateURL: '{{ cfyamls3url }}'

outputs:
  - CreateCloudFormationStack.StackId
  - CreateCloudFormationStack.StackStatus
  - CreateCloudFormationStack.StackStatusReason
  - CreateCloudFormationStack.OutputPayload

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws davis forecast for aws volume usage
workflows.guide:
## 📈 **Subworkflow for Forecasting AWS Volume Disk Usage**

🔮 This subworkflow provides a streamlined method for **forecasting disk usage of an AWS EBS volume** using Dynatrace Davis AI.

---

### 🕒 **Overview**

Since an AWS volume can only be extended **once every 6 hours**, this subworkflow estimates the **maximum expected disk usage within that time window**.  
📊 The forecasted value can then be passed to a workflow task responsible for extending the volume to the predicted size.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **host_entity_id** (Required): 🖥️ The Dynatrace Host entity ID associated with the AWS volume.

- **forecast_method** (Required): 📐 Specifies which forecast prediction to use.  
  Possible values:
  - **point** → `dt.davis.forecast:point`
  - **upper** → `dt.davis.forecast:upper`
  - **lower** → `dt.davis.forecast:lower`

  📚 More information about these methods:
  - https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/discover-dynatrace/platform/davis-ai/ai-models/forecast-analysis  
  - https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/stay-ahead-of-the-game-forecast-it-capacity-with-dynatrace-grail-and-davis-ai/

- **on_forecast_failure_increase_by_percentage_of_total_diskspace** (Required): 🛟  
  A fallback percentage of the total volume size used to calculate an extension size if:
  - forecasting fails due to insufficient data, or  
  - the forecasted value is smaller than the current volume size.

---

### 🔗 **Workflow Requirement**

Workflow needs subworkflow  
[subworkflow - aws get host data of ec2 instance](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/3e5454a4-50ad-4dab-914c-638b6df5b283?trigger=&view=live)

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- ▶️ When triggered, the subworkflow uses the **host_entity_id** to forecast disk usage **6 hours into the future**.
- ⚠️ If the forecast fails due to insufficient data **or** the predicted usage is less than the current volume size, the subworkflow performs an alternative calculation using the fallback parameter.

---

### 🔁 **Alternative Calculation**

If forecasting is unsuccessful, the volume is extended using the following formula:

workflows.guideSummary: 📈 Subworkflow for Forecasting AWS Volume Disk Usage
workflows.description:
Subworkflow: Forecasting AWS Volume Disk Usage
This subworkflow provides a streamlined method for forecasting the disk usage of an AWS EBS volume.

Since an AWS volume can only be extended once every 6 hours, this subworkflow estimates the maximum expected usage within that time window.
The forecasted value can then be passed to a workflow task responsible for extending the volume to the predicted size.

Parameters
host_entity_id (Required):
The Dynatrace Host entity ID associated with the AWS volume.

forecast_method (Required):
possible values are "point", "upper", "lower" depending on which of the prediction outcomes you would like to use
point = dt.davis.forecast:point
upper = dt.davis.forecast:upper
lower = dt.davis.forecast:lower
You can find more information about these methods in the documentation:
https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/discover-dynatrace/platform/davis-ai/ai-models/forecast-analysis
https://www.dynatrace.com/news/blog/stay-ahead-of-the-game-forecast-it-capacity-with-dynatrace-grail-and-davis-ai/

on_forecast_failure_increase_by_percentage_of_total_diskspace (Required):
A fallback percentage of the total volume size used to calculate an extension size if the forecast fails or returns a value smaller than the current volume size.

Workflow Logic
When triggered, the subworkflow uses the host_entity_id to forecast the disk usage 6 hours into the future.
If the forecast failsdue to insufficient data or if the predicted usage is less than the current volume sizethe subworkflow performs an alternative calculation using the fallback parameter.

Alternative Calculation
If forecasting is unsuccessful, the volume is extended using the following formula:
New Volume Size = Current Volume Size + (Current Volume Size  Fallback Percentage)

For example:
If the current volume size is 100 GB and the fallback percentage is 50%, the new volume size will be:
100 GB + (100 GB  0.50) = 150 GB

## 🗑️ **Subworkflow to Delete an AWS CloudFormation Stack**

🧹 This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to **trigger the deletion of an AWS CloudFormation stack** using AWS Systems Manager (SSM).

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **cf_stackname** (Required): 🏷️ The name of the CloudFormation stack to delete.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region in which the CloudFormation stack will be deleted.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used for authentication.

---

### 📘 **Prerequisites**

✅ This subworkflow requires the custom AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document  
**`Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack`** to be available in the AWS region provided by the `awsregion` parameter.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends a command to delete the CloudFormation stack to the  
  AWS SSM document **`Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack`**
- ⏳ It waits for the SSM document execution to complete
- ✅ The result is checked to verify that the deletion was successful
- ❌ If the deletion fails, the subworkflow fails to enable **restart-on-error** behavior in the parent workflow

---

### 🔗 **Workflow Requirement**

Workflow needs subworkflow  
[subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

   *(You must be logged in to the AWS Management Console.)*

2. 🌍 If needed, change the AWS region in the console.

3. ✏️ In the upper-left corner, change the runbook name to:  
   **`Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack`**

4. 🧾 Click the **`{}` Code** button next to the runbook name to open the YAML definition.

5. 📋 Paste the following YAML content into the code editor:

```yaml
schemaVersion: '0.3'

parameters:
  stackname:
    type: String

mainSteps:
  - name: DeleteCloudFormationStack
    action: aws:deleteStack
    isEnd: true
    inputs:
      StackName: '{{ stackname }}'

Subworkflow to Delete an AWS CloudFormation Stack

This subworkflow provides a simple way to trigger the deletion of an AWS CloudFormation stack 

Parameters:

cf_stackname: (Required) stackname of the cloudformation stack you want to delete
awsregion: (Required) region in which you want to delete the cloudformation stack

This subworkflow needs the custom SSM Document 
"Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack" available in the
region that is handed over by the parameter "awsregion" 

The subworkflow sends the command to delete a cloudformation stack to the
aws SSM document "Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack", then waits until
the SSM document has finished and then checks if the deletion was 
successful. If not, it will fail the subworkflow to provide a 
restart on error functionality to the Parent workflow

2.) If needed change the aws region of the Runbook in the console
3.) In the upper left corner Change the Runbook name into "Dynwf-DeleteCloudFormationStack"
4.) Click on the {} Code button next to the runbook name this will take you to the yaml def. of the runbook
5.) Paste the Yaml content below into the code field

# yaml file start

schemaVersion: '0.3'
description: |-

mainSteps:

## 🧹 **Subworkflow to Delete an Unused Non-Default AWS Security Group**

🔐 This subworkflow deletes a **non-default AWS security group** that is **not associated with any elastic network interface (ENI)**.

A **non-default security group** is defined as any security group whose name is **not** `default`.  
⚠️ If the security group ID passed to this subworkflow belongs to a **default security group**, **no changes will be made**.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awssecuritygroupid** (Required): 🆔 The AWS security group ID to delete.  
- **assumerolearn** (Required): 🎭 The AWS IAM role ARN to assume, which must allow deletion of security groups.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the security group resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWSConfigRemediation-DeleteUnusedSecurityGroup/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow validates that the specified security group:
  - is **not** a default security group, and  
  - is **not attached** to any elastic network interface (ENI)
- 📤 A delete command is sent to the AWS SSM document  
  **`AWSConfigRemediation-DeleteUnusedSecurityGroup`**
- ⏳ The subworkflow waits for execution to complete
- ✅ If the deletion is successful, the workflow completes
- ❌ If the deletion fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---
``

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws disable public access for security group
workflows.guide:
## 🚫 **Subworkflow to Disable SSH and RDP Public Access on an AWS Security Group**

🔐 This subworkflow disables **SSH (22)** and **RDP (3389)** ingress rules that are:
- opened to a **specific IP address**, or  
- opened to **all addresses (`0.0.0.0/0`)** if no IP address is specified.

⚠️ *The security group must already have existing ingress rules on the SSH and/or RDP ports for traffic to be disabled.*

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awssecuritygroupid** (Required): 🆔 The AWS security group ID for which traffic should be restricted.  
- **ipaddresstoblock** (Optional): 🌐 An IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR block to block access from  
  *(example: `1.2.3.4/32`)*.  
  If not provided, **all IP addresses** (including `0.0.0.0/0`) are considered.
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the security group resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ This subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 **AWS-DisablePublicAccessForSecurityGroup**

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow evaluates existing ingress rules on the specified security group
- 🚫 SSH (22) and RDP (3389) rules matching the specified IP address (or all addresses) are disabled
- 📤 The remediation is executed via the AWS SSM document
- ⏳ The subworkflow waits for execution to complete
- ✅ If successful, the workflow completes
- ❌ If execution fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

### 🔗 **Workflow Requirement**

This subworkflow Needs subworkflow  
[subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws ec2 start instance and verify running
workflows.guide:
## ▶️ **Subworkflow to Start an AWS EC2 Instance and Verify Running State**

🚀 This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to **start one or more stopped AWS EC2 instances** and **wait until they are fully in the `running` state** before continuing.

It is commonly used in remediation, recovery, and scale-out workflows where downstream steps must only execute once the instance is available.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceIds** (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID(s) to start.  
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 AWS region in which the EC2 instance(s) reside.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 Name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow first triggers the EC2 start operation, then waits until all target instances report state **`running`**.  
❌ If start or wait-state validation fails, the subworkflow fails so parent workflows can apply restart-on-error or alternate handling logic.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Useful for **auto-recovery and self-healing workflows**
- ✅ Useful for **scheduled start windows** in cost-optimized environments
- ✅ Ensures dependent tasks only run after instance availability is confirmed
- ✅ Integrates securely through the **Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection**

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws extend ebs volume
workflows.guide:
## 📦 **Subworkflow to Extend AWS Volumes (Once Every 6 Hours)**

📈 This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to **extend AWS EBS volumes** attached to an EC2 instance.  
⏱️ Since AWS volumes can only be extended **once every 6 hours**, this workflow is designed to safely handle volume and file system resizing.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **osType** (Required): 🖥️ The operating system type of the EC2 instance  
  *(Supported values: `Windows` or `Linux`)*

- **SizeGib** (Required): 📏 The target size to extend the volume to (in **GiB**).

- **VolumeId** (Required): 🆔 The ID of the EBS volume to be extended.

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the volume resides.

- **InstanceId** (Required): 🧾 The EC2 instance ID that the volume is attached to.

- **MountPoint** (Optional): 📂 The mount point of the partition to be extended  
  *(Required for Linux, e.g. `/`, `/data`)*

- **DriveLetter** (Optional): 💽 The drive letter to be extended  
  *(Required for Windows, e.g. `C:`)*

- **keepSnapShot** (Optional): 📸 Boolean flag indicating whether to retain the snapshot after a successful resize  
  *(Defaults to `true`)*

- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used for authentication.

- **AutomationAssumeRolearn** (Optional): 🎭 The ARN of the IAM role that allows Automation to perform actions on your behalf.

---

### ✅ **Requirements**

⚠️ This subworkflow **only works on SSM-managed AWS EC2 instances**.

📖 Refer to the AWS documentation below to ensure your EC2 instances are managed by AWS Systems Manager:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Documents Used**

This subworkflow requires the following **out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document**:

- **`aws-extendebsvolume`**  
  🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/aws-extendebsvolume.html

---

### 🧠 **Provided Functionality**

The **`aws-extendebsvolume`** SSM document performs the following actions:

- 📸 **Create a snapshot** of the volume *(optional)*  
- 📦 **Resize the EBS volume** at the AWS level  
- 🧩 **Resize partitions and file systems** on the operating system (Linux or Windows)

workflows.guideText:
📦 Subworkflow to Extend AWS Volumes (Once Every 6 Hours)

📈 This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to extend AWS EBS volumes attached to an EC2 instance.  
⏱️ Since AWS volumes can only be extended once every 6 hours, this workflow is designed to safely handle volume and file system resizing.

---
⚙️ Parameters
- osType (Required): 🖥️ The operating system type of the EC2 instance  
  (Supported values: Windows or Linux)
- SizeGib (Required): 📏 The target size to extend the volume to (in GiB).
- VolumeId (Required): 🆔 The ID of the EBS volume to be extended.
- awsregion (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the volume resides.
- InstanceId (Required): 🧾 The EC2 instance ID that the volume is attached to.
- MountPoint (Optional): 📂 The mount point of the partition to be extended  
  (Required for Linux, e.g. /, /data)
- DriveLetter (Optional): 💽 The drive letter to be extended  
  (Required for Windows, e.g. C:)
- keepSnapShot (Optional): 📸 Boolean flag indicating whether to retain the snapshot after a successful resize  
  (Defaults to true)
- dynatraceawsconnection (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used for authentication.
- AutomationAssumeRolearn (Optional): 🎭 The ARN of the IAM role that allows Automation to perform actions on your behalf.

---
✅ Requirements

⚠️ This subworkflow only works on SSM-managed AWS EC2 instances.

📖 Refer to the AWS documentation below to ensure your EC2 instances are managed by AWS Systems Manager:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---
📘 AWS SSM Documents Used

This subworkflow requires the following out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:
- aws-extendebsvolume  
  🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/aws-extendebsvolume.html

---
🧠 Provided Functionality

The aws-extendebsvolume SSM document performs the following actions:
- 📸 Create a snapshot of the volume (optional)  
- 📦 Resize the EBS volume at the AWS level  
- 🧩 Resize partitions and file systems on the operating system (Linux or Windows)

---

workflows.guide:
## 🔄 **Subworkflow to Reboot an AWS EC2 Instance**

♻️ This subworkflow provides a simple and reliable way to **reboot an AWS EC2 instance** and **wait until the instance is back online** before continuing.

It is commonly used as a remediation or recovery step in automation workflows where a controlled restart is required.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceIds** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID to reboot.  
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region in which the EC2 instance resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow triggers the EC2 reboot operation and **waits until the instance is fully back up and reachable**.  
❌ If the reboot operation fails, the subworkflow will fail to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Commonly used for **self-healing and remediation workflows**
- ✅ Ensures downstream steps only execute once the instance is available again
- ✅ Suitable for automation triggered by problems, events, or maintenance workflows
- ✅ Integrates securely using the **Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection**

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws ec2 resize instance type
workflows.guide:
## 📐 **Subworkflow to Resize an AWS EC2 Instance Type**

📐 This subworkflow provides a controlled way to **resize an AWS EC2 instance** by stopping it, changing the instance type, starting it again, and verifying it returns to **`running`** state.

It is commonly used for right-sizing, performance tuning, and cost optimization workflows when compute capacity must be adjusted safely.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID to resize.  
- **newInstanceType** (Required): 🏷️ Target EC2 instance type (for example: `t3.large`, `m5.xlarge`).  
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 AWS region in which the EC2 instance resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 Name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow follows a safe sequence: **stop instance -> wait for `stopped` -> modify instance type -> start instance -> wait for `running`**.  
❌ If any step in the sequence fails, the subworkflow fails so parent workflows can apply restart-on-error or alternate handling logic.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Useful for **automated right-sizing based on CPU/memory saturation signals**
- ✅ Useful for **planned performance upgrades or temporary scaling actions**
- ✅ Ensures downstream tasks execute only after the resized instance is healthy again
- ✅ Integrates securely through the **Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection**

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws run command on ec2 instance
workflows.guide:
## ▶️ **Subworkflow to Run a Command on an AWS EC2 Instance**

🖥️ This subworkflow provides an **easy and abstracted way** to execute a command on an **AWS EC2 instance** without requiring any knowledge of how AWS Systems Manager (SSM) works.

It triggers the execution of a command via AWS SSM, **waits until the command execution has completed**, and **validates whether the execution was successful**.  
❌ If the execution fails, the subworkflow will fail so the **parent workflow can restart on error**.

---

### ✅ **Requirements**

⚠️ In order for this subworkflow to execute successfully, the EC2 instance **must be a Systems Manager (SSM) managed instance**.

📖 AWS documentation:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **commands** (Required): 🧾 The command or script to run on the EC2 instance.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the EC2 instance resides.  
- **instanceid** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID on which the command will run.  
- **executionTimeout** (Required): ⏱️ The execution timeout for the command.  
- **workingDirectory** (Required): 📂 The working directory in which the command is executed.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWS-RunShellScript/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends the command to the AWS SSM document **`AWS-RunShellScript`**
- ⏳ It waits for the command execution to complete
- ✅ The execution result is checked for success
- ❌ If execution fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

This subworkflow Needs subworkflow  
🧩[subworkflow - aws wait for run command execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/97218693-adf0-4c82-a295-e43d848b0b4d)

workflows.guide:
## 🛡️ **Subworkflow to Block Public Access to an Amazon S3 Bucket**

🔒 This subworkflow provides a simple and standardized way to **block public access to an Amazon S3 bucket** by configuring S3 public access block settings.

It is commonly used as part of **security remediation workflows** to prevent unintended public exposure of S3 buckets.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the S3 bucket resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.  
- **BucketName** (Required): 🪣 The name of the S3 bucket *(not the ARN)*.

- **RestrictPublicBuckets** (Optional): 🚫  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should restrict public bucket policies.  
  When set to `true`, access is limited to AWS services and authorized users within the same account if the bucket has a public policy.

- **BlockPublicAcls** (Optional): 📵  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should block public access control lists (ACLs) for the bucket and its objects.

- **IgnorePublicAcls** (Optional): 🙈  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should ignore all public ACLs on the bucket and its objects.  
  When set to `true`, any existing public ACLs are ignored.

- **BlockPublicPolicy** (Optional): 🧱  
  Specifies whether Amazon S3 should block public bucket policies.  
  When set to `true`, Amazon S3 rejects calls to `PUT Bucket policy` if the policy allows public access.

- **AutomationAssumeRole** (Required): 🎭  
  The ARN of the IAM role that allows Automation to perform actions on your behalf.

- **StabilizationPeriod** (Optional): ⏱️  
  The duration (in seconds) to wait for remediation stabilization.  
  Must be between **1** and **604,799** seconds.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow evaluates the current public access configuration of the specified S3 bucket
- 🛡️ Public access settings are applied based on the provided parameters
- ✅ If successful, the bucket is protected against unintended public exposure
- ❌ If execution fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

workflows.guide:
## 🔍 **Subworkflow to Scan for Publicly Exposed AWS Security Group Ingress Rules**

🚨 This subworkflow scans AWS security groups for **ingress rule definitions with ports open to `0.0.0.0/0`**, exposing them to **the entire internet**, and **raises a custom Dynatrace alert** when such configurations are detected.

---

### ⏱️ **Why This Matters**

AWS Security Hub typically scans for these misconfigurations only every **12–24 hours**.  
⚡ This subworkflow provides a **more frequent and proactive alternative**, allowing you to detect and respond to insecure security group configurations much faster.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.  
- **regions_to_search** (Required): 🌍 One or more AWS regions to scan for security groups with ingress rules open to `0.0.0.0/0`.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 The subworkflow scans security groups across the specified AWS regions
- 🚫 Ingress rules allowing access from `0.0.0.0/0` are identified
- 🚨 A **custom Dynatrace alert** is raised when insecure configurations are detected
- ✅ Enables downstream workflows to remediate or notify based on the alert

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws ssm clear disk space on linux ec2
workflows.guide:
## 🧯 **Subworkflow to Clear Disk Space on Linux EC2 Instances**

🧯 This subworkflow provides a simple way to **free up disk space on Linux EC2 instances** by removing old log files, temporary files, and journal entries based on a configurable retention period.

It is commonly used in response to disk saturation alerts or as part of automated maintenance workflows to reclaim storage capacity without manual intervention.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID to run cleanup on.  
- **logRetentionDays** (Optional): 📅 Number of days to retain logs (default: 7). Logs and temp files older than this are deleted.  
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 AWS region where the EC2 instance resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 Name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow executes an SSM Run Command that removes logs and temp files older than the retention period, then clears systemd journal.  
❌ If the command execution fails, the subworkflow fails so parent workflows can handle the error and retry if needed.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Useful for **automated response to disk saturation alerts**
- ✅ Useful for **scheduled maintenance workflows** to reclaim storage
- ✅ Removes old logs (**/var/log**, **/tmp**, systemd journal) based on retention policy
- ✅ Integrates securely through the **Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection**

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws ssm delete ami image
workflows.guide:
## 🗑️ **Subworkflow to Delete AMI Image**

⚠️ This subworkflow **deletes AMI images and associated snapshots** via **AWS-DeleteImage** SSM automation.

**WARNING: This action removes AMI images from your account. Ensure they are no longer needed before deletion.**

Useful for cleaning up old AMI versions, managing image inventory, and reducing storage costs.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **imageId** (Required): 🆔 Primary AMI ID to delete.  
- **imageIds** (Optional): 🆔 Additional AMI IDs (comma-separated).  
- **snapshotIds** (Optional): 📸 Associated snapshot IDs to delete (comma-separated).  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document**

**Document Name**: `AWS-DeleteImage`

🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-deleteimage.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Deregisters AMI images from your account
- Deletes associated EBS snapshots
- Removes both public and private AMIs
- Prevents accidental reuse of old images
- Frees up storage space and reduces costs
- Cleans up image inventory

---

### 🔄 **Workflow Logic**

1. **Start Automation**: Triggers AWS-DeleteImage SSM document with image IDs and snapshot IDs
2. **Wait for Completion**: Polls the SSM automation status until complete (99 retries, 60s intervals)
3. **Check Status**: Validates the image deletion succeeded via JavaScript verification

---

### 💡 **Use Cases**

- 🧹 **Cleanup**: Remove outdated AMI versions
- 📊 **Inventory Management**: Clean up unused images
- 💰 **Cost Reduction**: Remove associated EBS snapshots to reduce storage costs
- 🔒 **Security**: Delete images no longer needed for security compliance
- 🔄 **Image Rotation**: Manage AMI lifecycle and retention
- 📈 **Space Management**: Reclaim storage from old images

---

### 🔗 Dependencies

This subworkflow depends on the following subworkflow:

[🧩 subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution](/ui/apps/dynatrace.automations/workflows/20661ec0-29d5-471f-9973-29679d5fe908)

workflows.guide:
## 🔄 **Subworkflow to Restart a Linux Systemd Service on EC2**

🔄 This subworkflow provides a simple way to **restart a systemd service on a Linux EC2 instance** via SSM Run Command and **verify the service reaches `active` state** after restart.

It is commonly used for service recovery, troubleshooting crashed services, or applying configuration changes that require service restart (nginx, httpd, tomcat, etc.).

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID where the service runs.  
- **serviceName** (Required): 📋 systemd service name to restart (e.g., `nginx`, `httpd`, `tomcat`, `ssh`, `mysql`).  
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 AWS region where the EC2 instance resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 Name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow restarts the specified service and verifies it reaches **`active`** state.  
❌ If the service fails to start or reach active state, the subworkflow fails so parent workflows can handle the error and retry if needed.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Useful for **service recovery workflows** triggered by service availability alerts
- ✅ Useful for **configuration management** when services must be restarted
- ✅ Ensures service health by verifying active state after restart
- ✅ Integrates securely through the **Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection**

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - aws ssm restart windows service on ec2
workflows.guide:
## 🔄 **Subworkflow to Restart a Windows Service on EC2**

🔄 This subworkflow provides a simple way to **restart a Windows service on a Windows EC2 instance** via SSM Run Command and **verify the service is back in `Running` state** after restart.

It is commonly used for service recovery, troubleshooting failed services, or applying configuration changes that require service restart (IIS/W3SVC, Windows Update, app services, agents).

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID that hosts the target Windows service.
- **serviceName** (Required): 📋 Windows service name to restart (for example, `W3SVC`, `wuauserv`, `Spooler`).
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 AWS region where the EC2 instance is running.
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection name used to authenticate the AWS API calls.

---

### ▶️ **Execution Behavior**

The subworkflow executes an SSM Run Command using **AWS-RunPowerShellScript** that runs `Restart-Service` for the provided service, then checks the service status with `Get-Service`.

If the service does not return to `Running`, the command fails with a clear error, and the workflow reports the failure to downstream automation.

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

Use this subworkflow when you need to:
- Recover from service failures on Windows-based workloads.
- Apply service-level configuration changes safely via automation.
- Restart infrastructure services during incident remediation.
- Validate service recovery before proceeding to dependent steps.

workflows.guide:
## 💻 **Subworkflow to Run a PowerShell Script on Windows EC2 and Wait**

💻 This subworkflow executes a **custom PowerShell script on a Windows EC2 instance** via SSM Run Command and then **waits for command completion** using a dedicated wait subworkflow.

It is useful for Windows remediation, diagnostics, service operations, and scripted maintenance tasks that must complete before downstream workflow steps continue.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID where the script will run.
- **powershellScript** (Required): 📋 PowerShell script content to execute (single or multi-command script).
- **awsRegion** (Required): 🌍 AWS region where the EC2 instance is running.
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection name used to authenticate AWS API calls.

---

### ▶️ **Execution Behavior**

The subworkflow first sends the script with **AWS-RunPowerShellScript** to the target instance, then calls a wait subworkflow to poll command execution until a terminal state is reached.

If command execution fails, times out, or returns a non-success state, this subworkflow returns a failure so downstream automation can stop or trigger fallback handling.

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

Use this subworkflow when you need to:
- Execute Windows remediation scripts during incident response.
- Run diagnostics and collect output before taking next actions.
- Perform scripted maintenance tasks on managed Windows EC2 hosts.
- Gate downstream workflow steps on confirmed script completion.

workflows.guideText:
🖥️ Subworkflow to Run a Shell Script on Linux EC2 and Wait

🖥️ This subworkflow executes a custom shell script on a Linux EC2 instance via SSM Run Command and then waits for command completion using a dedicated wait subworkflow.

It is useful for Linux remediation, diagnostics, ad-hoc maintenance, and scripted operations that must complete before downstream workflow steps continue (clearing disk space, restarting services, flushing caches, etc.).

---
⚙️ Parameters
- instanceId (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID where the script will run.
- shellScript (Required): 📋 Shell script content to execute (single or multi-line bash script).
- awsRegion (Required): 🌍 AWS region where the EC2 instance is running.
- dynatraceawsconnection (Required): 🔑 Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection name used to authenticate AWS API calls.

---
▶️ Execution Behavior

The subworkflow first sends the script with AWS-RunShellScript to the target Linux instance, then calls a wait subworkflow to poll command execution until a terminal state is reached.

If command execution fails, times out, or returns a non-success state, this subworkflow returns a failure so downstream automation can stop or trigger fallback handling.

---
🧩 Usage Context

Use this subworkflow when you need to:
- Execute Linux remediation scripts during incident response.
- Run diagnostics and collect output before taking next actions.
- Perform scripted maintenance tasks on managed Linux EC2 hosts.
- Gate downstream workflow steps on confirmed script completion.

workflows.guide:
## 🔄 **Subworkflow to Update an AWS CloudFormation Stack**

📦 This subworkflow provides a simple and controlled way to **trigger an update of an existing AWS CloudFormation stack** using AWS Systems Manager (SSM).

It is commonly used to apply **infrastructure changes, configuration updates, or template enhancements** in a standardized and automated way.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **cf_stackname** (Required): 🏷️ The name of the CloudFormation stack to update.  
- **cfyamls3url** (Required): 📄 The S3 URL of the CloudFormation YAML template to apply  
  *(must follow this format: `https://s3bucket.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/cf.yaml`)*  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the CloudFormation stack resides.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The name of the Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.  
- **assumerolearn** (Required): 🎭 The ARN of an IAM role that allows updating the CloudFormation stack.

---

### ⏳ **Execution Behavior**

⏱️ The subworkflow waits for the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) document execution to complete.  
❌ If the SSM document execution fails, this subworkflow will also fail, enabling **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

This subworkflow uses the out-of-the-box AWS Systems Manager document:

🔗 https://us-east-1.console.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/documents/AWS-UpdateCloudFormationStack/description?region=us-east-1

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow sends an update command to the AWS SSM document  
  **`AWS-UpdateCloudFormationStack`**
- ⏳ It waits for the SSM document execution to complete
- ✅ The execution result is validated to confirm the stack update was successful
- ❌ If the update fails, the subworkflow fails to allow **restart-on-error** behavior in the parent workflow

---

This subworkflow Needs subworkflow  
**subworkflow - aws wait for systems manager document execution**

workflows.guide:
## ⏳ **Subworkflow to Wait for AWS SSM Run Command Execution to Finish**

🧠 This subworkflow is used by the **“aws run command on EC2 instance”** subworkflow to **wait for an AWS SSM Run Command execution to complete** and **validate the execution status**.

✅ If the command execution is successful, the subworkflow completes.  
❌ If the execution fails, the subworkflow fails as well—allowing **parent workflows to react with restart-on-error or alternative handling**.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region in which the SSM Run Command is executed.  
- **awsinstanceid** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID on which the Run Command was executed.  
- **CommandId** (Required): 🧾 The command ID of the Run Command execution  
  *(returned by the AWS Run Command action)*.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection used to authenticate with AWS.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- ⏳ The subworkflow continuously checks the status of the specified Run Command execution
- ✅ If the execution completes successfully, the subworkflow finishes
- ❌ If the execution fails or returns an error state, the subworkflow fails
- 🔁 This enables **restart-on-error** or compensating logic in the parent workflow

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed as a **helper / control-flow subworkflow**
- ✅ Ensures downstream workflow steps only execute after command completion
- ✅ Commonly paired with:
  - Run command on EC2 instance
  - Remediation scripts
  - Validation and recovery workflows

---

workflows.guide:
## 🗑️ **Subworkflow to Delete All Workflows of a Specific Owner**

⚠️ This subworkflow provides a powerful way to **delete all workflows owned by a specific user**.

When the subworkflow starts, it:
- 👤 Determines the **owner of the running workflow**
- 🔍 Searches for **all workflows owned by that user**
- 🗑️ **Deletes every workflow it finds**

---

### 🚨 **Important Warning**

⚠️ **This action is destructive and irreversible.**

All workflows owned by the identified user will be **permanently deleted**.

✅ **Ensure workflows are backed up or exported before running this subworkflow.**

This subworkflow should only be used in **controlled governance, cleanup, or emergency scenarios**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🚫 Removing **unauthorized or non-compliant workflows**
- 🧼 Performing **bulk cleanup** during governance enforcement
- 🛡️ Responding to security or audit findings
- 🔁 Resetting environments during testing or enablement activities

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

🚫 This subworkflow **does not require any input parameters**.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 👤 Identify the owner of the executing workflow
- 🔍 Locate all workflows owned by that user
- 🗑️ Delete each workflow
- ✅ Complete if successful
- ❌ Fail if an error occurs, allowing the **parent workflow to respond appropriately**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow governance and lifecycle enforcement**
- ✅ Intended for **advanced administrative automation**
- ✅ Complements workflow create, export, and validation subworkflows
- ✅ Should be protected by approvals or access controls

---

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace check if problem is solved or closed
workflows.guide:
## ✅ **Subworkflow to Check if a Problem Is Solved or Closed**

🧠 This subworkflow is used to **validate whether a specific remediation step has resolved a problem**.

It is especially useful in workflows that execute **multiple remediation actions**, where you want to verify after each step whether the problem has been **solved or closed** before proceeding further.

✅ If the problem is solved or closed, the subworkflow completes successfully.  
❌ If the problem is still open, the subworkflow fails—allowing the **parent workflow to retry or take alternative action**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

When executing iterative remediation steps, this subworkflow enables **automated decision-making** based on the current state of a problem.

You can configure **retry-on-error** behavior in the parent workflow to:
- 🔁 Retry this subworkflow for a defined duration (for example, every minute for 10 minutes)
- ⏳ Periodically re-check whether the problem has been resolved

If the problem is **not resolved within the defined time window**, you can:

- ➕ Trigger an additional remediation step  
- 👤 Escalate the issue to a human operator  

This approach supports **automated, iterative problem resolution** while maintaining full control over escalation and fallback strategies.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **problem_display_id** (Required): 🆔 The problem display ID  
  *(for example: `P-25051158`)*

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed as a **control and validation subworkflow**
- ✅ Commonly used between remediation steps
- ✅ Enables retry-based logic instead of hard-coded delays
- ✅ Works seamlessly with **restart-on-error** patterns in parent workflows

---

## 🧩 **Subworkflow to Create a Workflow**

⚙️ This subworkflow provides a simple and automated way to **create a workflow from a JSON definition**.

It is commonly used to **store workflows as code** and enable **one‑button workflow imports** through automation—making it easier to manage workflows with **complex subworkflow dependencies**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 📦 Storing workflows in version control and importing them on demand  
- ▶️ Enabling **one‑click workflow deployment** through another workflow

workflows.guide:
## 🧩 **Subworkflow to Delete a Dynatrace User**

⚙️ This subworkflow provides a simple and automated way to **delete a Dynatrace user**.

It is commonly used to **off-board users** from a Dynatrace account and can be used in onboarding/offboarding requests,
for example from ServiceNow, Jira, or Zendesk.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🛑 Off-board users from a Dynatrace account

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **email** (Required): 📧 The **email address** of the user to be off-boarded.
- **dynatrace_account_id** (Required): 📧 The **account ID** of the account you would like to off-board the user from.
- **credential_vault_dynatrace_client_id** (Required): 🔒 The **Credential Vault name** of the credential vault entry that contains the Dynatrace OAuth client ID.
- **credential_vault_dynatrace_client_secret** (Required): 🔒 The **Credential Vault name** of the credential vault entry that contains the Dynatrace OAuth client secret.

---

### 🛠️ **Prerequisites**

Before running this workflow, Dynatrace OAuth credentials must be created with the following permissions:

- account-idm-read
- account-idm-write

The client ID and client secret must be stored in **separate Dynatrace Credential Vault entries**.

Once created, the names of the credential vault entries must be provided as input parameters to the workflow.

## 🗑️ **Subworkflow to Delete a Workflow**

🧹 This subworkflow provides a simple and automated way to **delete an existing workflow**.

It is commonly used for **governance and cleanup scenarios**, such as automatically removing **unauthorized, deprecated, or non-compliant workflows** from an environment.

✅ If the workflow is deleted successfully, the subworkflow completes.  
❌ If the deletion fails, the subworkflow fails—allowing the **parent workflow to retry or take alternative action**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🚫 Automatically deleting **unauthorized workflows**
- 🧼 Cleaning up obsolete or deprecated workflows
- 🛡️ Enforcing automation governance and compliance
- 🔁 Maintaining a controlled and standardized workflow environment

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **workflowid** (Required): 🆔 The ID of the workflow to be deleted.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow submits a delete request for the specified workflow ID
- 🗑️ The workflow is removed from the environment
- ✅ If deletion is successful, the subworkflow completes
- ❌ If deletion fails, the subworkflow fails to enable **restart-on-error** handling in the parent workflow

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow lifecycle management**
- ✅ Useful in governance, compliance, and cleanup workflows
- ✅ Complements workflow creation and export subworkflows
- ✅ Supports automation-at-scale best practices

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace export one or all workflows to existing github repo
workflows.guide:
## 📤 **Subworkflow to Export One or More Workflows to a GitHub Repository**

📦 This subworkflow provides an automated way to **export one or all workflows to a GitHub repository**.

It supports **workflow-as-code** practices by enabling version control, backup, governance, and promotion of workflows across environments.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🗂️ Backing up workflows to GitHub for safekeeping
- 🔁 Promoting workflows between environments (dev → test → prod)
- 🛡️ Supporting governance, audit, and compliance requirements
- 📦 Managing workflows as code using Git-based processes

This subworkflow can export:
- ✅ A **single workflow**, or  
- ✅ **all workflows**, depending on configuration and permissions

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **workflowId** (Optional): 🆔 The ID of a specific workflow to export  
  *(for example: `15508756-7ee1-4c03-ad1b-d3d95aca478f`)*  
  If left empty (`""`), **all workflows** will be exported.

- **searchLimit** (Required): 🔢 The maximum number of workflows to export.

- **githubRepoName** (Required): 📁 The name of the **existing GitHub repository** to export workflows to.

- **githubOwnerName** (Required): 👤 The GitHub **owner or organization name**.

- **githubRepoBranchName** (Required): 🌿 The GitHub **branch name** to export to.

- **dynatraceGithubConnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace GitHub connection name used for authentication.

- **adminAccess** (Required): 🛡️ Boolean flag (`true` / `false`) controlling export scope:
  - `false`: Only workflows the user has permission to access will be exported  
  - `true`: If the user has **workflow-admin** rights, **all workflows** will be exported

---

### 🔗 **GitHub Connection Setup**

Before using this subworkflow, ensure that a **GitHub Connector** is configured in Dynatrace.

📖 Official Dynatrace documentation:  
https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/workflows/actions/github/github-workflows-setup [1](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/analyze-explore-automate/workflows/actions/github/github-workflows-setup)

This guide explains how to:
- Configure outbound access to GitHub
- Create and authorize a GitHub connection
- Generate and scope a GitHub personal access token
- Make the connection available to workflows

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 🔍 Identify workflows based on `workflowId`, permissions, and `adminAccess`
- 📤 Export workflow definitions to the specified GitHub repository and branch
- ✅ Complete if export succeeds
- ❌ Fail if export encounters an error, allowing the **parent workflow to retry or react**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow lifecycle and governance automation**
- ✅ Enables Git-based workflow management
- ✅ Complements workflow create, delete, and validation subworkflows
- ✅ Supports automation-at-scale and CI/CD patterns

---

workflows.guide:
## 🧠 **Subworkflow to Get All Data of a Problem**

🔎 This subworkflow provides an easy way to **retrieve complete problem details** that are **not included in the standard problem trigger**.

Important information—such as **impact**, **affected users**, and other contextual fields—is often required to correctly determine the **priority of a problem** and to synchronize that priority with external tracking systems like **Jira** or **ServiceNow**.

This subworkflow makes it simple to access that additional data **without requiring JavaScript knowledge**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

A common use case is **enriching problem data before creating or updating tickets** in tracking tools.

In addition, this subworkflow includes logic to **set the priority of a Jira issue** based on the data retrieved from the problem card.

⚙️ This logic may vary between environments, but it can be easily adjusted in the  
`// calculate jira impact level` section of the **get‑problem‑info** task.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **event.id** (Required): 🆔 The Dynatrace event ID associated with the problem.

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed as a **data‑enrichment subworkflow**
- ✅ Provides access to full problem context beyond trigger payloads
- ✅ Enables accurate prioritization in Jira, ServiceNow, and other tools
- ✅ Commonly used early in remediation or escalation workflows

---

- email (Required): 📧 The email address of the user to be on-boarded.
- dynatrace_account_id (Required): 📧 The account ID of the account you would like to on-board the user to.
- credential_vault_dynatrace_client_id (Required): 🔒 The Credential Vault name of the credential vault entry that contains the Dynatrace OAuth client ID.
- credential_vault_dynatrace_client_secret (Required): 🔒 The Credential Vault name of the credential vault entry that contains the Dynatrace OAuth client secret.

---
🛠️ Prerequisites

Before running this workflow, Dynatrace OAuth credentials must be created with the following permissions:
- account-idm-read
- account-idm-write

The client ID and client secret must be stored in separate Dynatrace Credential Vault entries.

Once created, the names of the credential vault entries must be provided as input parameters to the workflow.

✍️ This subworkflow provides a simple and automated way to **send text prompts to OpenAI** and use the generated output directly within a workflow.

It is commonly used for **communication, content generation, and response automation** based on operational events or workflow context.

workflows.title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace set all workflows of owner to draft
workflows.guide:
## 🧩 **Subworkflow to Set All Workflows of the Owner to Draft**

🛠️ This subworkflow provides an automated way to **set all workflows owned by the current workflow owner to draft**.

When the subworkflow is started, it:
- 👤 Identifies the **owner of the running workflow**
- 🔍 Searches for **all workflows owned by that user**
- 📝 Transitions each workflow into **draft state**

This is especially useful for **governance, bulk control, or emergency rollback scenarios**.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

Typical use cases include:

- 🚫 Temporarily disabling all workflows owned by a specific user
- 🧼 Bulk workflow control during governance or audit activities
- 🛡️ Preventing execution of workflows during incidents or investigations
- 🔁 Pausing automation before making bulk changes or cleanups

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

🚫 This subworkflow **does not require any input parameters**.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 👤 Determine the owner of the executing workflow
- 🔍 Locate all workflows owned by that user
- 📝 Set each workflow to **draft**
- ✅ Complete if successful
- ❌ Fail if an error occurs, allowing the **parent workflow to retry or take alternative action**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Designed for **workflow governance and lifecycle control**
- ✅ Enables centralized bulk actions
- ✅ Complements workflow create, delete, and export subworkflows
- ✅ Supports automation-at-scale best practices

---

## **🧩dynatrace create coe subworkflows**

⚙️ This workflow provides a simple and automated way to **create all coe subworkflows in your tenant**.

* All created subworkflows are created in the tenant **under the actor user executing the workflow**, so the actor user becomes the **owner and actor** of each created subworkflow.
 
* If a subworkflow already exists, it is **skipped** and not recreated.
 
* Simply run the workflow to create subworkflows in your tenant. 
* User that runs the workflow must have workflow write permissions

## 📋 **Subworkflow to Copy AMI Image**

🔀 This subworkflow **copies or encrypts an AMI across AWS regions** via **AWS-CopyImage** SSM automation.

Useful for DR, multi-region deployments, and encrypting unencrypted images.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **sourceImageId** (Required): 🆔 Source AMI ID to copy.  
- **sourceRegion** (Required): 🌍 Source AWS region.  
- **targetRegion** (Required): 🌍 Target AWS region.  
- **imageName** (Optional): 📝 Name for copied AMI.  
- **imageDescription** (Optional): 📋 Description.  
- **encrypted** (Optional): 🔐 Encrypt the copied AMI (true/false).  
- **kmsKeyId** (Optional): 🔑 KMS key ID for encryption.

- Copies an AMI to a different region
- Optionally encrypts the copied AMI
- Copies all snapshots associated with the AMI
- Enables multi-region deployment

workflows.title: subworkflow - aws ssm create rds snapshot
workflows.guide:
## 💾 **Subworkflow to Create RDS Snapshot**

✅ This subworkflow **creates automated snapshots of RDS database instances** via **AWS-CreateDBSnapshot** SSM automation.

Useful for database backups, point-in-time recovery, and creating database clones for testing and analysis.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **dbInstanceIdentifier** (Required): 🆔 The RDS DB instance identifier to snapshot.  
- **dBSnapshotIdentifier** (Optional): 📝 Custom identifier for the snapshot.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document**

**Document Name**: `AWS-CreateDBSnapshot`

🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-createdbsnapshot.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Creates a snapshot of an RDS database instance
- Captures database state at point-in-time
- Snapshots are stored in AWS backup infrastructure
- Can be used for point-in-time recovery or database cloning
- Supports all RDS engine types (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server)
- Non-disruptive backup process

---

### 🔄 **Workflow Logic**

1. **Start Automation**: Triggers AWS-CreateDBSnapshot SSM document with DB instance identifier
2. **Wait for Completion**: Polls the SSM automation status until complete (99 retries, 60s intervals)
3. **Check Status**: Validates the snapshot creation succeeded via JavaScript verification

---

### 💡 **Use Cases**

- 🔐 **Backup Strategy**: Regular snapshots for disaster recovery
- ⏮️ **Point-in-Time Recovery**: Restore to any snapshot
- 🔄 **Database Cloning**: Create test/dev databases from production snapshots
- 📊 **Data Analysis**: Isolated environments for analytics
- 🔀 **Cross-Region Replication**: Copy snapshots for DR in other regions
- 🛡️ **Compliance**: Automated backup audit trails

---

workflows.guide:
## 🏷️ **Subworkflow to Create Resource Tags**

✅ This subworkflow **adds tags to AWS resources** via **AWS-CreateResourceTags** SSM automation.

Useful for cost allocation, resource organization, automation workflows, and maintaining resource metadata across infrastructure.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **resourceId** (Required): 🆔 Resource ID to be tagged (instance, volume, snapshot, etc).  
- **resourceType** (Required): 📋 Type of resource (instance, volume, snapshot, image, etc).  
- **tags** (Required): 🏷️ Tags as key-value JSON object or query string format.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document**

**Document Name**: `AWS-CreateResourceTags`

🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-createresourcetags.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Adds tags to EC2 instances, volumes, AMIs, snapshots, and other resources
- Supports multiple resource types
- Enables cost allocation tagging
- Facilitates automated resource discovery and management
- Supports tag-based access control (TBAC)
- Batch tagging across multiple resources

---

### 🔄 **Workflow Logic**

1. **Start Automation**: Triggers AWS-CreateResourceTags SSM document with resource ID and tags
2. **Wait for Completion**: Polls the SSM automation status until complete (99 retries, 60s intervals)
3. **Check Status**: Validates the tagging succeeded via JavaScript verification

---

### 💡 **Use Cases**

- 💰 **Cost Allocation**: Tag resources for billing center tracking
- 🎯 **Resource Organization**: Organize resources by project, environment, team
- 🔍 **Automated Discovery**: Find resources by tags for automation
- 🔐 **Access Control**: Implement tag-based access control (TBAC)
- 📊 **Lifecycle Management**: Tag resources for retention policies
- 🛡️ **Compliance**: Tag resources for compliance frameworks
- 🤖 **Automation Workflows**: Use tags as triggers for automation

---

workflows.guide:
## 📸 **Subworkflow to Create EBS Snapshot**

✅ This subworkflow **creates point-in-time snapshots of EBS volumes** via **AWS-CreateSnapshot** SSM automation.

Useful for data protection, backup strategies, and creating copies of volumes for analysis or replication.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **volumeId** (Required): 🆔 The EBS volume ID to snapshot.  
- **description** (Optional): 📝 Snapshot description for identification.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document**

**Document Name**: `AWS-CreateSnapshot`

🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-createsnapshot.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Creates a point-in-time snapshot of an EBS volume
- Captures all data on the volume at snapshot time
- Snapshots are stored in Amazon S3
- Can be used to create new volumes or restore data
- Enables disaster recovery and data backup strategies

---

### 🔄 **Workflow Logic**

1. **Start Automation**: Triggers AWS-CreateSnapshot SSM document with specified volume ID
2. **Wait for Completion**: Polls the SSM automation status until complete (99 retries, 60s intervals)
3. **Check Status**: Validates the snapshot creation succeeded via JavaScript verification

---

### 💡 **Use Cases**

- 📦 **Data Protection**: Create backups before major changes
- 🔄 **Volume Replication**: Snapshot source for creation of new volumes
- 📊 **Analysis**: Create snapshots for forensic or performance analysis
- 🛡️ **Disaster Recovery**: Regular snapshots for business continuity
- 🔀 **Data Migration**: Snapshots for moving data between accounts/regions

---

workflows.title: subworkflow - aws ssm enable ebs encryption
workflows.guide:
## 🔐 **Subworkflow to Enable EBS Encryption**

✅ This subworkflow **enables encryption on EBS volumes** via **AWS-EnableEBSEncryption** SSM automation.

Useful for compliance requirements, data protection, and securing storage across infrastructure without downtime.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **snapshotId** (Required): 🆔 Source EBS snapshot ID to create encrypted volume from.  
- **kmsKeyId** (Optional): 🔑 KMS key ID for encryption (uses default AWS key if not specified).  
- **availabilityZone** (Required): 🌍 Availability zone for the encrypted volume.  
- **encrypted** (Optional): 🔐 Enable encryption (true/false, default: true).  
- **volumeType** (Optional): 📦 EBS volume type (gp2, gp3, io1, io2, st1, sc1).  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document**

**Document Name**: `AWS-EnableEBSEncryption`

🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-enable-encryption.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Creates encrypted volume from unencrypted snapshot
- Supports custom KMS keys for encryption
- Maintains data integrity during encryption
- Works with all EBS volume types
- Enables compliance with encryption policies
- Supports AWS KMS customer-managed keys

---

### 🔄 **Workflow Logic**

1. **Start Automation**: Triggers AWS-EnableEBSEncryption SSM document with snapshot and encryption settings
2. **Wait for Completion**: Polls the SSM automation status until complete (99 retries, 60s intervals)
3. **Check Status**: Validates the encryption setup succeeded via JavaScript verification

---

### 💡 **Use Cases**

- 🔐 **Compliance**: Meet encryption requirements (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, etc)
- 🛡️ **Security**: Encrypt unencrypted volumes
- 🔑 **KMS Key Management**: Use organization-specific KMS keys
- 📊 **Data Protection**: Encrypt production data at rest
- 🔄 **Volume Replication**: Encrypt volumes during migration
- 📈 **Performance**: Optimize with encrypted high-IOPS volumes (io2)

---

workflows.title: subworkflow - aws ssm run patch baseline
workflows.guide:
## 🔧 **Subworkflow to Run Patch Baseline**

✅ This subworkflow **scans and applies patches to EC2 instances** via **AWS-RunPatchBaseline** SSM automation.

Useful for keeping instances patched, maintaining compliance, and automating security updates across infrastructure.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 EC2 instance ID to scan/patch.  
- **baselineId** (Optional): 📋 Patch baseline ID (uses default if not specified).  
- **snapshotId** (Optional): 📸 Snapshot ID for patch baseline scan results.  
- **operation** (Optional): 🔄 Operation type - Scan or Install (default: Scan).  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document**

**Document Name**: `AWS-RunPatchBaseline`

🔗 https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-runpatchbaseline.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Scans EC2 instances for missing patches
- Reports on patch compliance
- Can install patches automatically
- Supports multiple operating systems (Windows, Linux)
- Works with AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager
- Generates detailed patch scan results
- Enables patch compliance reporting

---

### 🔄 **Workflow Logic**

1. **Start Automation**: Triggers AWS-RunPatchBaseline SSM document with instance ID and operation
2. **Wait for Completion**: Polls the SSM automation status until complete (99 retries, 60s intervals)
3. **Check Status**: Validates the patch baseline execution succeeded via JavaScript verification

---

### 💡 **Use Cases**

- 🛡️ **Security Patching**: Find and install security updates
- 📊 **Compliance Scanning**: Assess patch compliance across instances
- 🔄 **Automated Patching**: Schedule regular patch cycles
- 📋 **Patch Reporting**: Generate patch status reports
- 🔐 **CVE Management**: Apply CVE-related patches
- 🏢 **Multi-Instance Patching**: Patch fleets of instances

---
